Software Development Manager – AI
Platform
Architecture & AI Coding Challenge
Part 1: AI Coding Exercise
The Problem
Build (primarily using AI coding tools) a local invoice-processing agent exposed via an
HTTP API that:
1. Accepts a folder of images containing invoices
2. Extracts relevant invoice information
3. Summarizes costs by expense category
4. Streams agent progress, trajectory, and final results via Server-Sent Events (SSE)
5. Produces observable, reviewable execution traces
Use of AI Coding Tools (Required)
You are explicitly required to use any local AI coding tools you prefer, including:
• GitHub Copilot
• Claude Code
• Cursor
• ChatGPT
• Other AI-assisted development tools
We want to see:
• How you leverage AI productively
• Where you apply judgment
• How you validate or correct AI-generated code
• Where AI should not be trusted in this workflow
This is not a test of manual coding speed. It is a test of system design, correctness, and
use of AI tools.
Timebox
2–3 hours total. Do not over-engineer.
Allowed Technology
• Preferred: Python + FastAPI or Node + Express
• You may use any LLM of choice, whether locally or cloud hosted
• Local execution only
• LocalStack (or comparable Docker runtime)
• You may use an industry-standard agent framework such as:
o LangChain
o Strands
o Claude Code
If you choose not to use a framework, briefly explain why in DESIGN.md.
OCR/VLM/LLM usage may be real or mocked (mock mode recommended).
Functional Requirements
Your agent must produce:
• Total spend across all invoices
• Spend by expense category
• Per-invoice structured output:
 Vendor
 Invoice date
 Invoice number (if present)
 Total
 Assigned category
 Confidence or notes
• A short “Issues & Assumptions” section
Example allowed categories
• Travel (air/hotel)
• Meals & Entertainment
• Software / Subscriptions
• Professional Services
• Office Supplies
• Shipping / Postage
• Utilities
• Other (must include explanatory note)
You may introduce internal subcategories, but final output must map to one of the above.
What to Build
1) HTTP Streaming Endpoint (Required)
POST /runs/stream
This endpoint must:
• Start a new invoice-processing run
• Immediately return an SSE stream
• Stream execution events until completion or failure
Request
• multipart/form-data with one or more invoice images and optional prompt, or
• JSON body with a local folder path and optional prompt
The optional prompt should influence agent behavior (e.g., conservative categorization,
flag unusual invoices, etc.).
Streaming Requirements
The server must stream:
• run_started
• progress
• tool_call
• tool_result
• invoice_result
• final_result
• error (if applicable)
The stream remains open until:
• All invoices are processed and final_result is emitted, or an unrecoverable error
occurs
2) Constrained Tool System & Guardrails (Required)
Your agent must operate through a clearly defined tool registry (4–6 tools).
The agent may only access invoice data via these tools.
Example tools (you may rename/adjust as you see fit):
1. load_images(input) -> [image_refs]
2. extract_invoice_fields(image_ref) -> structured_fields
3. normalize_invoice(fields) -> normalized_fields
4. categorize_invoice(normalized_fields, allowed_categories) -> {category,
confidence, notes}
5. aggregate(invoices) -> totals
6. generate_report(aggregates, invoices, issues) -> final_output
3) Required Output
The final structured output must include:
Run-Level Summary
• Total spend across all invoices
• Spend by expense category
• “Issues & Assumptions” section
Per-Invoice Structured Output
• Vendor
• Invoice date
• Invoice number (if present)
• Total
• Assigned category
• Confidence or notes
4) Tracing & Observability
Each run must produce a reviewable trace of execution.
You may implement this via:
• Structured logs (JSON recommended), and/or
• A third-party tracing tool (Langfuse, LangSmith, OpenTelemetry, etc.)
At minimum, the trace must capture:
• Step boundaries (extraction, normalization, categorization, aggregation, reporting)
• Tool calls and summarized inputs/outputs
• Planner decisions (high-level summaries)
• Errors or retries
• Timing information
The trace should allow a reviewer to understand:
• What the agent did
• In what order
• Why the final output was produced
Deliverables
Submit a single repository containing:
README.md with prerequisites, setup instructions, how to run the API, a curl example for
POST /runs/stream, example SSE output, and mock mode instructions (if implemented).
spec.md defining the /runs/stream contract, SSE event schema, tool registry and
schemas, and final output schema.
A /transcripts/ folder containing raw AI coding tool interaction logs.
Your source code implementing the streaming endpoint, tool registry, agent orchestration,
trajectory streaming, structured output, and tracing/observability.
DESIGN.md (max 1 page) summarizing your architecture, framework choice (if any),
planner/execution/state separation, model selection rationale, and what you would
improve for production.
Include at least one sample run trace showing execution steps and final output.
If applicable, include any agent configuration files (e.g., AGENTS.md, MCP setup, tool
authorization configuration) reflecting your AI-assisted development setup.
Follow-Up
You will participate in a live discussion to:
• Walk through architectural decisions
• Explore tradeoffs under evolving constraints
• Defend implementation boundaries
• Discuss how you would guide engineers building this system

content.thinking:
The project is a blank slate - just the task specification. Now I need to deeply analyze the requirements and ask the user clarifying questions before I can create a solid plan.

Let me analyze the spec carefully:

**Core requirements:**
1. `POST /runs/stream` - SSE streaming endpoint
2. Accept: multipart/form-data (images) OR JSON (folder path) + optional prompt
3. Tool registry: 4-6 tools (load_images, extract_invoice_fields, normalize_invoice, categorize_invoice, aggregate, generate_report)
4. SSE events: run_started, progress, tool_call, tool_result, invoice_result, final_result, error


5. Output: per-invoice structured data + run-level summary
6. Tracing: JSON structured logs or third-party tool

Before I can build this, I need to clarify several key decisions with the user: the tech stack (Python/FastAPI or Node/Express), which LLM to integrate (OpenAI, Anthropic, local, or mocked), what agent framework to use, whether OCR/VLM should be real or mocked, and how to handle tracing.

I'm also wondering about the LocalStack requirement mentioned in the spec—it's typically for mocking AWS services, but for invoice processing it's unclear if we need it for S3-like storage or if plain Docker is sufficient. Given the 2–3 hour timeframe and the recommendation to avoid over-engineering, I should probably lean toward mock mode and clarify these architectural choices upfront.

content.text: The project is a clean slate — just the spec. I've fully analyzed it. Before planning, I need to nail down several key architectural decisions with you.

questions.question: Which tech stack do you want to use?

options.label: Python + FastAPI
options.description: Native async SSE support, excellent LangChain/LLM ecosystem, type-safe with Pydantic. Best fit for AI agent work. (Recommended)

options.label: Node + Express
options.description: JavaScript ecosystem, easier SSE with EventEmitter, familiar if you prefer JS.

questions.question: Which LLM should power the invoice extraction and categorization?

options.label: Claude API (Anthropic)
options.description: claude-sonnet-4-6 for vision + text. Strong structured output, vision-capable for reading invoice images directly. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

options.label: OpenAI GPT-4o
options.description: GPT-4o vision + structured outputs. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY.

options.label: Mock mode only
options.description: No real LLM calls — deterministic fake responses. Fast, free, no API key needed. Good for demonstrating the architecture without cost.

options.label: Local model (Ollama)
options.description: Run a local vision model (e.g., llava, mistral). Zero cloud cost, but slower and requires Ollama installed.

questions.question: Which agent framework do you want to use (or none)?

options.label: Custom orchestrator (no framework)
options.description: Pure Python — a hand-rolled tool registry and planner loop. Minimal deps, full control, easy to explain. Best for showing deep understanding. (Recommended)

options.label: LangChain / LangGraph
options.description: Mature framework with built-in tool calling, tracing via LangSmith, and graph-based execution flow.

options.label: Strands Agents (AWS)
options.description: AWS-native agent SDK. Newer and less commonly known, but listed in the spec.

questions.question: How should tracing & observability be implemented?

options.label: Structured JSON logs to file
options.description: Write a JSONL trace file per run to a /traces/ directory. Minimal deps, fully local, easy to review. (Recommended)

options.label: Langfuse (self-hosted)
options.description: Rich UI for trace inspection. Requires running Langfuse via Docker locally.

options.label: LangSmith
options.description: Cloud-based LangChain tracing. Requires LANGSMITH_API_KEY and internet access.

input.content:
# Local Invoice-Processing Agent — Implementation Plan

## Context

This is a take-home coding challenge for a Software Development Manager – Platform role. The goal is to build a local invoice-processing agent exposed via an HTTP API that accepts uploaded invoice images, extracts and categorizes expense data, streams progress via SSE, and produces structured output with full observability. The challenge explicitly requires use of AI coding tools and is timeboxed at 2–3 hours.

**Choices made:**
- Python + FastAPI (async-native, ideal for SSE + AI ecosystem)
- LangGraph (StateGraph with fan-out/fan-in for parallel invoice processing)
- Claude API `claude-sonnet-4-6` via `langchain-anthropic` (vision + structured output)
- LangSmith tracing + local JSONL trace files per run
- Mock mode via `MOCK_MODE=true` env var
- `uv` for package management
- Dockerfile + docker-compose for local execution
- Multipart/form-data file upload (input mode)
- No LocalStack — pure local filesystem

---

## Project Structure

```
invoice-agent/
├── pyproject.toml           # uv project definition + dependencies
├── uv.lock
├── .env.example
├── .gitignore
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
├── main.py                  # FastAPI app entry + uvicorn runner
├── config.py                # pydantic-settings (env vars, model names, categories)
├── agent/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── state.py             # InvoiceProcessingState TypedDict + all Pydantic models
│   ├── tools.py             # 6-tool registry (callable functions)
│   ├── graph.py             # LangGraph StateGraph definition
│   ├── llm.py               # Claude LLM client factory (real + mock toggle)
│   └── mock.py              # Deterministic mock LLM responses
├── api/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── routes.py            # POST /runs/stream SSE endpoint
│   └── events.py            # SSE event types, formatters, queue-based emitter
├── traces/                  # Per-run JSONL trace files (gitignored)
├── transcripts/             # AI coding tool interaction logs (placeholder)
└── samples/                 # User drops invoice images here (gitignored)
```

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Project Scaffolding
**Files:** `pyproject.toml`, `.env.example`, `.gitignore`, `config.py`

- Init uv project with all dependencies:
  - `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `python-multipart`
  - `sse-starlette` (SSE StreamingResponse)
  - `anthropic`, `langchain`, `langgraph`, `langchain-anthropic`, `langsmith`
  - `pydantic`, `pydantic-settings`, `python-dotenv`
- `config.py` uses `pydantic-settings` to read env vars:
  - `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MOCK_MODE` (bool, default false)
  - `LANGCHAIN_API_KEY`, `LANGCHAIN_PROJECT`, `LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2`
  - `ALLOWED_CATEGORIES` (list, the 8 canonical categories)

### Step 2: Data Models & State
**File:** `agent/state.py`

Pydantic models:
```python
ImageRef(path: str, filename: str, size_bytes: int)
ExtractedFields(vendor: str | None, date: str | None, invoice_number: str | None,
                total: float | None, currency: str, raw_text: str)
NormalizedInvoice(vendor: str, date: date | None, invoice_number: str | None,
                  total_usd: float, currency: str)
CategorizedInvoice(NormalizedInvoice + category: str, confidence: float, notes: str)
Aggregates(total_spend: float, by_category: dict[str, float], invoice_count: int)
FinalReport(run_id: str, summary: Aggregates, invoices: list[CategorizedInvoice],
            issues: list[str], generated_at: datetime)
```

LangGraph state:
```python
class InvoiceProcessingState(TypedDict):
    run_id: str
    prompt: str | None
    image_refs: list[ImageRef]
    processed_invoices: Annotated[list[CategorizedInvoice], add]  # fan-in reducer
    aggregates: Aggregates | None
    final_report: FinalReport | None
    issues: Annotated[list[str], add]
    error: str | None
```

### Step 3: Tool Registry (6 tools)
**File:** `agent/tools.py`

All tools are plain async Python functions (NOT LangChain `@tool` decorated — called directly by graph nodes to keep control flow explicit and SSE-friendly):

1. `load_images(files) -> list[ImageRef]` — save uploads to temp dir, return refs
2. `extract_invoice_fields(image_ref, llm) -> ExtractedFields` — Claude vision API call with structured output prompt; reads image as base64
3. `normalize_invoice(fields) -> NormalizedInvoice` — pure Python: parse dates, strip currency symbols, clean vendor names, flag missing fields → issues list
4. `categorize_invoice(invoice, categories, prompt_hint, llm) -> CategorizedInvoice` — Claude text call: "Given this invoice, assign one category from [list]. Return JSON with category, confidence (0-1), notes."
5. `aggregate(invoices) -> Aggregates` — pure Python: sum totals, group by category
6. `generate_report(aggregates, invoices, issues, run_id) -> FinalReport` — pure Python: assemble final structured output

### Step 4: Mock Mode
**File:** `agent/mock.py`

When `MOCK_MODE=true`:
- `mock_extract(image_ref)` → returns rotating fake invoices (Acme Corp, $1,240 travel; TechConf, $89 software; etc.)
- `mock_categorize(invoice)` → keyword-match on vendor name → deterministic category + 0.95 confidence
- Same tool function signatures — `llm.py` factory switches between real Claude calls and mock functions

### Step 5: LangGraph State Machine
**File:** `agent/graph.py`

```
START
  → load_images_node         (tool: load_images → populate image_refs)
  → dispatch_invoices_node   (returns Send("process_invoice", {...}) for each image)
  → [parallel] process_invoice_node  (extract → normalize → categorize one image)
                                     (appends to processed_invoices via reducer)
  → aggregate_node           (tool: aggregate)
  → generate_report_node     (tool: generate_report)
END
```

Fan-out/fan-in via LangGraph `Send` API:
```python
def dispatch_invoices(state) -> list[Send]:
    return [Send("process_invoice", {"image_ref": ref, ...}) for ref in state["image_refs"]]
```

Each graph node:
- Emits SSE events by writing to an `asyncio.Queue` passed via graph config (`RunnableConfig`)
- Records timing for trace
- Calls the appropriate tool function

### Step 6: SSE Events & Streaming Endpoint
**Files:** `api/events.py`, `api/routes.py`

Event types (dataclasses + `model_dump_json()`):
```
run_started    → {run_id, timestamp, invoice_count, mock_mode}
progress       → {run_id, step, message, current, total}
tool_call      → {run_id, tool, input_summary}       # input_summary: no raw pixels
tool_result    → {run_id, tool, output_summary, duration_ms}
invoice_result → {run_id, index, filename, data: CategorizedInvoice}
final_result   → {run_id, summary: Aggregates, invoices: [...], issues: [...]}
error          → {run_id, message, step, recoverable: bool}
```

SSE format (NDJSON inside SSE):
```
data: {"event": "run_started", ...}\n\n
```

`POST /runs/stream` endpoint:
1. Accept `files: list[UploadFile]`, optional `prompt: str = Form(None)`
2. Create `asyncio.Queue` for events
3. Build initial state, inject queue into `RunnableConfig`
4. Launch `graph.ainvoke(state, config)` as background asyncio task
5. `EventSourceResponse` generator: pulls from queue, yields SSE, stops on `final_result` or `error`
6. Write JSONL trace file to `traces/{run_id}.jsonl` as events flow

### Step 7: Tracing & Observability
**Dual approach:**

A. **LangSmith** (when `LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true`): automatic via LangChain/LangGraph integration. No extra code needed — just env vars.

B. **Local JSONL trace** (always on): Every SSE event + timing + full tool I/O appended to `traces/{run_id}.jsonl`. This is the "sample run trace" deliverable.

Trace schema per line:
```json
{"type": "step_start|step_end|tool_call|tool_result|error", "step": "...",
 "run_id": "...", "timestamp": "ISO", "duration_ms": 123, "data": {...}}
```

### Step 8: Docker
**Files:** `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml`

- `Dockerfile`: Python 3.12-slim, install uv, copy project, `uv sync`, expose port 8000
- `docker-compose.yml`: single service, env_file `.env`, volume mount for `./traces` and `./samples`

### Step 9: Documentation
**Files:** `README.md`, `spec.md`, `DESIGN.md`

**README.md** must include:
- Prerequisites (Python 3.12+, uv, Docker, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- Setup: `uv sync` + `.env` configuration
- Run: `uvicorn main:app` OR `docker compose up`
- curl example: `curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/runs/stream -F "files=@invoice1.png" -F "prompt=conservative categorization"`
- Example SSE output (from a sample run)
- Mock mode: `MOCK_MODE=true uvicorn main:app`

**spec.md** must include:
- `/runs/stream` request/response contract
- Full SSE event schema (all 7 event types with JSON examples)
- Tool registry (each tool: name, input schema, output schema, LLM-dependent: yes/no)
- Final output schema

**DESIGN.md** (max 1 page):
- Architecture: FastAPI → LangGraph fan-out/fan-in → Claude vision + text → LangSmith
- Framework rationale: LangGraph for explicit state + parallel fan-out; LangSmith for built-in trace UI
- Planner/execution/state separation
- Model rationale: claude-sonnet-4-6 (vision + structured output quality)
- Mock mode rationale
- What to improve for production (async job queue, persistent storage, auth, rate limiting)

### Step 10: Sample Trace & Transcripts
- `traces/sample_run.jsonl` — pre-generated sample trace showing a 3-invoice run
- `transcripts/` — includes this planning session log + implementation session notes

---

## Critical Files Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `agent/state.py` | All data models + LangGraph state |
| `agent/tools.py` | 6-tool registry (core business logic) |
| `agent/graph.py` | LangGraph StateGraph (fan-out/fan-in) |
| `agent/llm.py` | Claude client factory + mock toggle |
| `api/routes.py` | SSE streaming endpoint |
| `api/events.py` | Event types + queue-based emitter |
| `main.py` | FastAPI app + router registration |
| `config.py` | All configuration via env vars |

---

## SSE Event Flow (Happy Path, 3 Invoices)

```
→ run_started         (count: 3)
→ tool_call           (load_images)
→ tool_result         (load_images, 3 refs)
→ progress            (dispatching 3 invoices in parallel)
→ [x3] tool_call      (extract_invoice_fields)
→ [x3] tool_result    (extract_invoice_fields)
→ [x3] tool_call      (normalize_invoice)
→ [x3] tool_result    (normalize_invoice)
→ [x3] tool_call      (categorize_invoice)
→ [x3] tool_result    (categorize_invoice)
→ [x3] invoice_result (per-invoice structured output)
→ tool_call           (aggregate)
→ tool_result         (aggregate)
→ tool_call           (generate_report)
→ tool_result         (generate_report)
→ final_result        (full structured output)
[stream closes]
```

---

## Verification Plan

1. **Mock mode smoke test**: `MOCK_MODE=true uvicorn main:app` → `curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/runs/stream -F "files=@any.png"` → verify all 7 SSE event types appear in order, stream closes after `final_result`
2. **Real mode test**: Set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, upload 2-3 real invoice images → verify extracted fields are accurate, categories map to allowed list, totals add up correctly
3. **Docker test**: `docker compose up` → same curl → same results
4. **LangSmith trace**: Check LangSmith dashboard shows run with all nodes and tool calls
5. **Local trace**: Verify `traces/{run_id}.jsonl` exists with all step boundaries, timing, tool I/O
6. **Parallel verification**: Upload 5 invoices → confirm multiple `extract_invoice_fields` tool_call events appear interleaved (not sequential) in SSE stream

toolUseResult.content:
# Local Invoice-Processing Agent — Implementation Plan

## Context

This is a take-home coding challenge for a Software Development Manager – Platform role. The goal is to build a local invoice-processing agent exposed via an HTTP API that accepts uploaded invoice images, extracts and categorizes expense data, streams progress via SSE, and produces structured output with full observability. The challenge explicitly requires use of AI coding tools and is timeboxed at 2–3 hours.

**Choices made:**
- Python + FastAPI (async-native, ideal for SSE + AI ecosystem)
- LangGraph (StateGraph with fan-out/fan-in for parallel invoice processing)
- Claude API `claude-sonnet-4-6` via `langchain-anthropic` (vision + structured output)
- LangSmith tracing + local JSONL trace files per run
- Mock mode via `MOCK_MODE=true` env var
- `uv` for package management
- Dockerfile + docker-compose for local execution
- Multipart/form-data file upload (input mode)
- No LocalStack — pure local filesystem

---

## Project Structure

```
invoice-agent/
├── pyproject.toml           # uv project definition + dependencies
├── uv.lock
├── .env.example
├── .gitignore
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
├── main.py                  # FastAPI app entry + uvicorn runner
├── config.py                # pydantic-settings (env vars, model names, categories)
├── agent/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── state.py             # InvoiceProcessingState TypedDict + all Pydantic models
│   ├── tools.py             # 6-tool registry (callable functions)
│   ├── graph.py             # LangGraph StateGraph definition
│   ├── llm.py               # Claude LLM client factory (real + mock toggle)
│   └── mock.py              # Deterministic mock LLM responses
├── api/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── routes.py            # POST /runs/stream SSE endpoint
│   └── events.py            # SSE event types, formatters, queue-based emitter
├── traces/                  # Per-run JSONL trace files (gitignored)
├── transcripts/             # AI coding tool interaction logs (placeholder)
└── samples/                 # User drops invoice images here (gitignored)
```

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Project Scaffolding
**Files:** `pyproject.toml`, `.env.example`, `.gitignore`, `config.py`

- Init uv project with all dependencies:
  - `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `python-multipart`
  - `sse-starlette` (SSE StreamingResponse)
  - `anthropic`, `langchain`, `langgraph`, `langchain-anthropic`, `langsmith`
  - `pydantic`, `pydantic-settings`, `python-dotenv`
- `config.py` uses `pydantic-settings` to read env vars:
  - `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MOCK_MODE` (bool, default false)
  - `LANGCHAIN_API_KEY`, `LANGCHAIN_PROJECT`, `LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2`
  - `ALLOWED_CATEGORIES` (list, the 8 canonical categories)

### Step 2: Data Models & State
**File:** `agent/state.py`

Pydantic models:
```python
ImageRef(path: str, filename: str, size_bytes: int)
ExtractedFields(vendor: str | None, date: str | None, invoice_number: str | None,
                total: float | None, currency: str, raw_text: str)
NormalizedInvoice(vendor: str, date: date | None, invoice_number: str | None,
                  total_usd: float, currency: str)
CategorizedInvoice(NormalizedInvoice + category: str, confidence: float, notes: str)
Aggregates(total_spend: float, by_category: dict[str, float], invoice_count: int)
FinalReport(run_id: str, summary: Aggregates, invoices: list[CategorizedInvoice],
            issues: list[str], generated_at: datetime)
```

LangGraph state:
```python
class InvoiceProcessingState(TypedDict):
    run_id: str
    prompt: str | None
    image_refs: list[ImageRef]
    processed_invoices: Annotated[list[CategorizedInvoice], add]  # fan-in reducer
    aggregates: Aggregates | None
    final_report: FinalReport | None
    issues: Annotated[list[str], add]
    error: str | None
```

### Step 3: Tool Registry (6 tools)
**File:** `agent/tools.py`

All tools are plain async Python functions (NOT LangChain `@tool` decorated — called directly by graph nodes to keep control flow explicit and SSE-friendly):

1. `load_images(files) -> list[ImageRef]` — save uploads to temp dir, return refs
2. `extract_invoice_fields(image_ref, llm) -> ExtractedFields` — Claude vision API call with structured output prompt; reads image as base64
3. `normalize_invoice(fields) -> NormalizedInvoice` — pure Python: parse dates, strip currency symbols, clean vendor names, flag missing fields → issues list
4. `categorize_invoice(invoice, categories, prompt_hint, llm) -> CategorizedInvoice` — Claude text call: "Given this invoice, assign one category from [list]. Return JSON with category, confidence (0-1), notes."
5. `aggregate(invoices) -> Aggregates` — pure Python: sum totals, group by category
6. `generate_report(aggregates, invoices, issues, run_id) -> FinalReport` — pure Python: assemble final structured output

### Step 4: Mock Mode
**File:** `agent/mock.py`

When `MOCK_MODE=true`:
- `mock_extract(image_ref)` → returns rotating fake invoices (Acme Corp, $1,240 travel; TechConf, $89 software; etc.)
- `mock_categorize(invoice)` → keyword-match on vendor name → deterministic category + 0.95 confidence
- Same tool function signatures — `llm.py` factory switches between real Claude calls and mock functions

### Step 5: LangGraph State Machine
**File:** `agent/graph.py`

```
START
  → load_images_node         (tool: load_images → populate image_refs)
  → dispatch_invoices_node   (returns Send("process_invoice", {...}) for each image)
  → [parallel] process_invoice_node  (extract → normalize → categorize one image)
                                     (appends to processed_invoices via reducer)
  → aggregate_node           (tool: aggregate)
  → generate_report_node     (tool: generate_report)
END
```

Fan-out/fan-in via LangGraph `Send` API:
```python
def dispatch_invoices(state) -> list[Send]:
    return [Send("process_invoice", {"image_ref": ref, ...}) for ref in state["image_refs"]]
```

Each graph node:
- Emits SSE events by writing to an `asyncio.Queue` passed via graph config (`RunnableConfig`)
- Records timing for trace
- Calls the appropriate tool function

### Step 6: SSE Events & Streaming Endpoint
**Files:** `api/events.py`, `api/routes.py`

Event types (dataclasses + `model_dump_json()`):
```
run_started    → {run_id, timestamp, invoice_count, mock_mode}
progress       → {run_id, step, message, current, total}
tool_call      → {run_id, tool, input_summary}       # input_summary: no raw pixels
tool_result    → {run_id, tool, output_summary, duration_ms}
invoice_result → {run_id, index, filename, data: CategorizedInvoice}
final_result   → {run_id, summary: Aggregates, invoices: [...], issues: [...]}
error          → {run_id, message, step, recoverable: bool}
```

SSE format (NDJSON inside SSE):
```
data: {"event": "run_started", ...}\n\n
```

`POST /runs/stream` endpoint:
1. Accept `files: list[UploadFile]`, optional `prompt: str = Form(None)`
2. Create `asyncio.Queue` for events
3. Build initial state, inject queue into `RunnableConfig`
4. Launch `graph.ainvoke(state, config)` as background asyncio task
5. `EventSourceResponse` generator: pulls from queue, yields SSE, stops on `final_result` or `error`
6. Write JSONL trace file to `traces/{run_id}.jsonl` as events flow

### Step 7: Tracing & Observability
**Dual approach:**

A. **LangSmith** (when `LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true`): automatic via LangChain/LangGraph integration. No extra code needed — just env vars.

B. **Local JSONL trace** (always on): Every SSE event + timing + full tool I/O appended to `traces/{run_id}.jsonl`. This is the "sample run trace" deliverable.

Trace schema per line:
```json
{"type": "step_start|step_end|tool_call|tool_result|error", "step": "...",
 "run_id": "...", "timestamp": "ISO", "duration_ms": 123, "data": {...}}
```

### Step 8: Docker
**Files:** `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml`

- `Dockerfile`: Python 3.12-slim, install uv, copy project, `uv sync`, expose port 8000
- `docker-compose.yml`: single service, env_file `.env`, volume mount for `./traces` and `./samples`

### Step 9: Documentation
**Files:** `README.md`, `spec.md`, `DESIGN.md`

**README.md** must include:
- Prerequisites (Python 3.12+, uv, Docker, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- Setup: `uv sync` + `.env` configuration
- Run: `uvicorn main:app` OR `docker compose up`
- curl example: `curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/runs/stream -F "files=@invoice1.png" -F "prompt=conservative categorization"`
- Example SSE output (from a sample run)
- Mock mode: `MOCK_MODE=true uvicorn main:app`

**spec.md** must include:
- `/runs/stream` request/response contract
- Full SSE event schema (all 7 event types with JSON examples)
- Tool registry (each tool: name, input schema, output schema, LLM-dependent: yes/no)
- Final output schema

**DESIGN.md** (max 1 page):
- Architecture: FastAPI → LangGraph fan-out/fan-in → Claude vision + text → LangSmith
- Framework rationale: LangGraph for explicit state + parallel fan-out; LangSmith for built-in trace UI
- Planner/execution/state separation
- Model rationale: claude-sonnet-4-6 (vision + structured output quality)
- Mock mode rationale
- What to improve for production (async job queue, persistent storage, auth, rate limiting)

### Step 10: Sample Trace & Transcripts
- `traces/sample_run.jsonl` — pre-generated sample trace showing a 3-invoice run
- `transcripts/` — includes this planning session log + implementation session notes

---

## Critical Files Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `agent/state.py` | All data models + LangGraph state |
| `agent/tools.py` | 6-tool registry (core business logic) |
| `agent/graph.py` | LangGraph StateGraph (fan-out/fan-in) |
| `agent/llm.py` | Claude client factory + mock toggle |
| `api/routes.py` | SSE streaming endpoint |
| `api/events.py` | Event types + queue-based emitter |
| `main.py` | FastAPI app + router registration |
| `config.py` | All configuration via env vars |

---

## SSE Event Flow (Happy Path, 3 Invoices)

```
→ run_started         (count: 3)
→ tool_call           (load_images)
→ tool_result         (load_images, 3 refs)
→ progress            (dispatching 3 invoices in parallel)
→ [x3] tool_call      (extract_invoice_fields)
→ [x3] tool_result    (extract_invoice_fields)
→ [x3] tool_call      (normalize_invoice)
→ [x3] tool_result    (normalize_invoice)
→ [x3] tool_call      (categorize_invoice)
→ [x3] tool_result    (categorize_invoice)
→ [x3] invoice_result (per-invoice structured output)
→ tool_call           (aggregate)
→ tool_result         (aggregate)
→ tool_call           (generate_report)
→ tool_result         (generate_report)
→ final_result        (full structured output)
[stream closes]
```

---

## Verification Plan

1. **Mock mode smoke test**: `MOCK_MODE=true uvicorn main:app` → `curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/runs/stream -F "files=@any.png"` → verify all 7 SSE event types appear in order, stream closes after `final_result`
2. **Real mode test**: Set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, upload 2-3 real invoice images → verify extracted fields are accurate, categories map to allowed list, totals add up correctly
3. **Docker test**: `docker compose up` → same curl → same results
4. **LangSmith trace**: Check LangSmith dashboard shows run with all nodes and tool calls
5. **Local trace**: Verify `traces/{run_id}.jsonl` exists with all step boundaries, timing, tool I/O
6. **Parallel verification**: Upload 5 invoices → confirm multiple `extract_invoice_fields` tool_call events appear interleaved (not sequential) in SSE stream
7. **Error case**: Upload a non-image file → verify `error` SSE event is emitted, stream closes gracefully

```
│   ├── state.py             # InvoiceProcessingState TypedDict + all Pydantic models
│   ├── tools.py             # 6-tool registry (callable functions)
│   ├── graph.py             # LangGraph StateGraph definition
│   ├── llm.py               # Claude LLM client factory (real + mock toggle)
│   └── mock.py              # Deterministic mock LLM responses
├── api/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── routes.py            # POST /runs/stream SSE endpoint
│   └── events.py            # SSE event types, formatters, queue-based emitter
├── traces/                  # Per-run JSONL trace files (gitignored)
├── transcripts/             # AI coding tool interaction logs (placeholder)
└── samples/                 # User drops invoice images here (gitignored)
```

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Project Scaffolding
**Files:** `pyproject.toml`, `.env.example`, `.gitignore`, `config.py`

- Init uv project with all dependencies:
  - `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `python-multipart`
  - `sse-starlette` (SSE StreamingResponse)
  - `anthropic`, `langchain`, `langgraph`, `langchain-anthropic`, `langsmith`
  - `pydantic`, `pydantic-settings`, `python-dotenv`
- `config.py` uses `pydantic-settings` to read env vars:
  - `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MOCK_MODE` (bool, default false)
  - `LANGCHAIN_API_KEY`, `LANGCHAIN_PROJECT`, `LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2`
  - `ALLOWED_CATEGORIES` (list, the 8 canonical categories)

### Step 2: Data Models & State
**File:** `agent/state.py`

Pydantic models:
```python
ImageRef(path: str, filename: str, size_bytes: int)
ExtractedFields(vendor: str | None, date: str | None, invoice_number: str | None,
                total: float | None, currency: str, raw_text: str)
NormalizedInvoice(vendor: str, date: date | None, invoice_number: str | None,
                  total_usd: float, currency: str)
CategorizedInvoice(NormalizedInvoice + category: str, confidence: float, notes: str)
Aggregates(total_spend: float, by_category: dict[str, float], invoice_count: int)
FinalReport(run_id: str, summary: Aggregates, invoices: list[CategorizedInvoice],
            issues: list[str], generated_at: datetime)
```

LangGraph state:
```python
class InvoiceProcessingState(TypedDict):
    run_id: str
    prompt: str | None
    image_refs: list[ImageRef]
    processed_invoices: Annotated[list[CategorizedInvoice], add]  # fan-in reducer
    aggregates: Aggregates | None
    final_report: FinalReport | None
    issues: Annotated[list[str], add]
    error: str | None
```

### Step 3: Tool Registry (6 tools)
**File:** `agent/tools.py`

All tools are plain async Python functions (NOT LangChain `@tool` decorated — called directly by graph nodes to keep control flow explicit and SSE-friendly):

1. `load_images(files) -> list[ImageRef]` — save uploads to temp dir, return refs
2. `extract_invoice_fields(image_ref, llm) -> ExtractedFields` — Claude vision API call with structured output prompt; reads image as base64
3. `normalize_invoice(fields) -> NormalizedInvoice` — pure Python: parse dates, strip currency symbols, clean vendor names, flag missing fields → issues list
4. `categorize_invoice(invoice, categories, prompt_hint, llm) -> CategorizedInvoice` — Claude text call: "Given this invoice, assign one category from [list]. Return JSON with category, confidence (0-1), notes."
5. `aggregate(invoices) -> Aggregates` — pure Python: sum totals, group by category
6. `generate_report(aggregates, invoices, issues, run_id) -> FinalReport` — pure Python: assemble final structured output

### Step 4: Mock Mode
**File:** `agent/mock.py`

When `MOCK_MODE=true`:
- `mock_extract(image_ref)` → returns rotating fake invoices (Acme Corp, $1,240 travel; TechConf, $89 software; etc.)
- `mock_categorize(invoice)` → keyword-match on vendor name → deterministic category + 0.95 confidence
- Same tool function signatures — `llm.py` factory switches between real Claude calls and mock functions

### Step 5: LangGraph State Machine
**File:** `agent/graph.py`

```
START
  → load_images_node         (tool: load_images → populate image_refs)
  → dispatch_invoices_node   (returns Send("process_invoice", {...}) for each image)
  → [parallel] process_invoice_node  (extract → normalize → categorize one image)
                                     (appends to processed_invoices via reducer)
  → aggregate_node           (tool: aggregate)
  → generate_report_node     (tool: generate_report)
END
```

Fan-out/fan-in via LangGraph `Send` API:
```python
def dispatch_invoices(state) -> list[Send]:
    return [Send("process_invoice", {"image_ref": ref, ...}) for ref in state["image_refs"]]
```

Each graph node:
- Emits SSE events by writing to an `asyncio.Queue` passed via graph config (`RunnableConfig`)
- Records timing for trace
- Calls the appropriate tool function

### Step 6: SSE Events & Streaming Endpoint
**Files:** `api/events.py`, `api/routes.py`

Event types (dataclasses + `model_dump_json()`):
```
run_started    → {run_id, timestamp, invoice_count, mock_mode}
progress       → {run_id, step, message, current, total}
tool_call      → {run_id, tool, input_summary}       # input_summary: no raw pixels
tool_result    → {run_id, tool, output_summary, duration_ms}
invoice_result → {run_id, index, filename, data: CategorizedInvoice}
final_result   → {run_id, summary: Aggregates, invoices: [...], issues: [...]}
error          → {run_id, message, step, recoverable: bool}
```

SSE format (NDJSON inside SSE):
```
data: {"event": "run_started", ...}\n\n
```

`POST /runs/stream` endpoint:
1. Accept `files: list[UploadFile]`, optional `prompt: str = Form(None)`
2. Create `asyncio.Queue` for events
3. Build initial state, inject queue into `RunnableConfig`
4. Launch `graph.ainvoke(state, config)` as background asyncio task
5. `EventSourceResponse` generator: pulls from queue, yields SSE, stops on `final_result` or `error`
6. Write JSONL trace file to `traces/{run_id}.jsonl` as events flow

### Step 7: Tracing & Observability
**Dual approach:**

A. **LangSmith** (when `LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true`): automatic via LangChain/LangGraph integration. No extra code needed — just env vars.

B. **Local JSONL trace** (always on): Every SSE event + timing + full tool I/O appended to `traces/{run_id}.jsonl`. This is the "sample run trace" deliverable.

Trace schema per line:
```json
{"type": "step_start|step_end|tool_call|tool_result|error", "step": "...",
 "run_id": "...", "timestamp": "ISO", "duration_ms": 123, "data": {...}}
```

### Step 8: Docker
**Files:** `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml`

- `Dockerfile`: Python 3.12-slim, install uv, copy project, `uv sync`, expose port 8000
- `docker-compose.yml`: single service, env_file `.env`, volume mount for `./traces` and `./samples`

### Step 9: Documentation
**Files:** `README.md`, `spec.md`, `DESIGN.md`

**README.md** must include:
- Prerequisites (Python 3.12+, uv, Docker, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- Setup: `uv sync` + `.env` configuration
- Run: `uvicorn main:app` OR `docker compose up`
- curl example: `curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/runs/stream -F "files=@invoice1.png" -F "prompt=conservative categorization"`
- Example SSE output (from a sample run)
- Mock mode: `MOCK_MODE=true uvicorn main:app`

**spec.md** must include:
- `/runs/stream` request/response contract
- Full SSE event schema (all 7 event types with JSON examples)
- Tool registry (each tool: name, input schema, output schema, LLM-dependent: yes/no)
- Final output schema

**DESIGN.md** (max 1 page):
- Architecture: FastAPI → LangGraph fan-out/fan-in → Claude vision + text → LangSmith
- Framework rationale: LangGraph for explicit state + parallel fan-out; LangSmith for built-in trace UI
- Planner/execution/state separation
- Model rationale: claude-sonnet-4-6 (vision + structured output quality)
- Mock mode rationale
- What to improve for production (async job queue, persistent storage, auth, rate limiting)

### Step 10: Sample Trace & Transcripts
- `traces/sample_run.jsonl` — pre-generated sample trace showing a 3-invoice run
- `transcripts/` — includes this planning session log + implementation session notes

---

## Critical Files Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `agent/state.py` | All data models + LangGraph state |
| `agent/tools.py` | 6-tool registry (core business logic) |
| `agent/graph.py` | LangGraph StateGraph (fan-out/fan-in) |
| `agent/llm.py` | Claude client factory + mock toggle |
| `api/routes.py` | SSE streaming endpoint |
| `api/events.py` | Event types + queue-based emitter |
| `main.py` | FastAPI app + router registration |
| `config.py` | All configuration via env vars |

---

## SSE Event Flow (Happy Path, 3 Invoices)

```
→ run_started         (count: 3)
→ tool_call           (load_images)
→ tool_result         (load_images, 3 refs)
→ progress            (dispatching 3 invoices in parallel)
→ [x3] tool_call      (extract_invoice_fields)
→ [x3] tool_result    (extract_invoice_fields)
→ [x3] tool_call      (normalize_invoice)
→ [x3] tool_result    (normalize_invoice)
→ [x3] tool_call      (categorize_invoice)
→ [x3] tool_result    (categorize_invoice)
→ [x3] invoice_result (per-invoice structured output)
→ tool_call           (aggregate)
→ tool_result         (aggregate)
→ tool_call           (generate_report)
→ tool_result         (generate_report)
→ final_result        (full structured output)
[stream closes]
```

---

## Verification Plan

1. **Mock mode smoke test**: `MOCK_MODE=true uvicorn main:app` → `curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/runs/stream -F "files=@any.png"` → verify all 7 SSE event types appear in order, stream closes after `final_result`
2. **Real mode test**: Set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, upload 2-3 real invoice images → verify extracted fields are accurate, categories map to allowed list, totals add up correctly
3. **Docker test**: `docker compose up` → same curl → same results
4. **LangSmith trace**: Check LangSmith dashboard shows run with all nodes and tool calls
5. **Local trace**: Verify `traces/{run_id}.jsonl` exists with all step boundaries, timing, tool I/O
6. **Parallel verification**: Upload 5 invoices → confirm multiple `extract_invoice_fields` tool_call events appear interleaved (not sequential) in SSE stream
7. **Error case**: Upload a non-image file → verify `error` SSE event is emitted, stream closes gracefully

toolUseResult.plan:
# Local Invoice-Processing Agent — Implementation Plan

## Context

This is a take-home coding challenge for a Software Development Manager – Platform role. The goal is to build a local invoice-processing agent exposed via an HTTP API that accepts uploaded invoice images, extracts and categorizes expense data, streams progress via SSE, and produces structured output with full observability. The challenge explicitly requires use of AI coding tools and is timeboxed at 2–3 hours.

**Choices made:**
- Python + FastAPI (async-native, ideal for SSE + AI ecosystem)
- LangGraph (StateGraph with fan-out/fan-in for parallel invoice processing)
- Claude API `claude-sonnet-4-6` via `langchain-anthropic` (vision + structured output)
- LangSmith tracing + local JSONL trace files per run
- Mock mode via `MOCK_MODE=true` env var
- `uv` for package management
- Dockerfile + docker-compose for local execution
- Multipart/form-data file upload (input mode)
- No LocalStack — pure local filesystem

---

## Project Structure

```

├── pyproject.toml           # uv project definition + dependencies
├── uv.lock
├── .env.example
├── .gitignore
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
├── main.py                  # FastAPI app entry + uvicorn runner
├── config.py                # pydantic-settings (env vars, model names, categories)
```

input.content:
[project]
name = "invoice-agent"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Local invoice-processing agent with SSE streaming"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "fastapi>=0.115.0",
    "uvicorn[standard]>=0.32.0",
    "python-multipart>=0.0.12",
    "sse-starlette>=2.1.3",
    "anthropic>=0.40.0",
    "langchain>=0.3.0",
    "langgraph>=0.2.0",
    "langchain-anthropic>=0.3.0",
    "langsmith>=0.1.0",
    "pydantic>=2.9.0",
    "pydantic-settings>=2.6.0",
    "python-dotenv>=1.0.0",
    "Pillow>=11.0.0",
    "aiofiles>=24.1.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "httpx>=0.27.0",
    "pytest>=8.3.0",
    "pytest-asyncio>=0.24.0",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]

from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
from pydantic import Field


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=".env", env_file_encoding="utf-8", extra="ignore")

    # Claude
    anthropic_api_key: str = Field(default="", alias="ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")

    # Mock mode — skip all LLM calls
    mock_mode: bool = Field(default=False, alias="MOCK_MODE")

    # LangSmith (optional)
    langchain_tracing_v2: str = Field(default="false", alias="LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2")
    langchain_api_key: str = Field(default="", alias="LANGCHAIN_API_KEY")
    langchain_project: str = Field(default="invoice-agent", alias="LANGCHAIN_PROJECT")

    # Server
    host: str = Field(default="0.0.0.0", alias="HOST")
    port: int = Field(default=8000, alias="PORT")

    # Model
    extraction_model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
    categorization_model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6"

    # Allowed expense categories (canonical list from spec)
    allowed_categories: list[str] = [
        "Travel (air/hotel)",
        "Meals & Entertainment",
        "Software / Subscriptions",
        "Professional Services",
        "Office Supplies",
        "Shipping / Postage",
        "Utilities",
        "Other",
    ]


settings = Settings()

"""
Data models and LangGraph state for the invoice-processing agent.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import operator
from datetime import date, datetime
from typing import Annotated, Optional
from uuid import uuid4

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


# ── Domain Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class ImageRef(BaseModel):
    """Reference to a saved invoice image."""
    ref_id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()))
    path: str
    filename: str
    size_bytes: int
    media_type: str = "image/png"


class ExtractedFields(BaseModel):
    """Raw fields extracted from an invoice image by Claude vision."""
    vendor: Optional[str] = None
    invoice_date: Optional[str] = None       # raw string as read from image
    invoice_number: Optional[str] = None
    total: Optional[float] = None
    currency: str = "USD"
    line_items: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    raw_notes: str = ""                      # anything else Claude noticed


class NormalizedInvoice(BaseModel):
    """Cleaned & normalized invoice fields."""
    image_ref: ImageRef
    vendor: str
    invoice_date: Optional[date] = None
    invoice_number: Optional[str] = None
    total_usd: float
    currency: str
    line_items: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


class CategorizedInvoice(BaseModel):
    """Fully processed invoice with expense category."""
    image_ref: ImageRef
    vendor: str
    invoice_date: Optional[date] = None
    invoice_number: Optional[str] = None
    total_usd: float
    currency: str
    category: str
    confidence: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    notes: str


class Aggregates(BaseModel):
    """Run-level spend summary."""
    total_spend: float
    by_category: dict[str, float]
    invoice_count: int


class FinalReport(BaseModel):
    """Complete structured output for a processing run."""
    run_id: str
    summary: Aggregates
    invoices: list[CategorizedInvoice]
    issues: list[str]
    generated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)


# ── LangGraph State ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class InvoiceProcessingState(dict):
    """
    LangGraph state dictionary.

    Annotations on list fields use operator.add so fan-in results
    from parallel process_invoice nodes are accumulated correctly.
    """
    run_id: str
    prompt: Optional[str]
    image_refs: list[ImageRef]
    # Annotated with operator.add → parallel fan-in reducer
    processed_invoices: Annotated[list[CategorizedInvoice], operator.add]
    issues: Annotated[list[str], operator.add]
    aggregates: Optional[Aggregates]
    final_report: Optional[FinalReport]
    error: Optional[str]


def make_initial_state(run_id: str, prompt: Optional[str] = None) -> dict:
    return {
        "run_id": run_id,
        "prompt": prompt,
        "image_refs": [],
        "processed_invoices": [],
        "issues": [],
        "aggregates": None,
        "final_report": None,
        "error": None,
    }

from datetime import date, datetime

input.content:
"""
Tool registry — the 6 tools the agent may use to process invoices.

All tools are plain async functions. The agent graph calls them directly
so we keep full control of SSE event emission timing.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import base64
import json
import os
import re
import tempfile
from datetime import date, datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import aiofiles
from fastapi import UploadFile

from agent.state import (
    Aggregates,
    CategorizedInvoice,
    ExtractedFields,
    FinalReport,
    ImageRef,
    NormalizedInvoice,
)


# ── Tool 1: load_images ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def load_images(files: list[UploadFile], tmp_dir: str) -> list[ImageRef]:
    """
    Save uploaded files to a temp directory and return ImageRef objects.
    """
    refs: list[ImageRef] = []
    for upload in files:
        data = await upload.read()
        filename = upload.filename or "invoice.png"
        dest = Path(tmp_dir) / filename
        # Handle name collisions
        counter = 1
        while dest.exists():
            stem, suffix = os.path.splitext(filename)
            dest = Path(tmp_dir) / f"{stem}_{counter}{suffix}"
            counter += 1

        async with aiofiles.open(dest, "wb") as f:
            await f.write(data)

        refs.append(ImageRef(
            path=str(dest),
            filename=dest.name,
            size_bytes=len(data),
            media_type=upload.content_type or "image/png",
        ))
    return refs


# ── Tool 2: extract_invoice_fields ────────────────────────────────────────────

EXTRACTION_PROMPT = """You are an invoice data extractor. Analyze the invoice image and return a JSON object with these fields:
{
  "vendor": "Company name on the invoice",
  "invoice_date": "Date as YYYY-MM-DD or null",
  "invoice_number": "Invoice/receipt number or null",
  "total": numeric total amount (float) or null,
  "currency": "3-letter ISO currency code, default USD",
  "line_items": ["list of line item descriptions"],
  "raw_notes": "anything unusual or ambiguous you noticed"
}
Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences."""


async def extract_invoice_fields(
    image_ref: ImageRef,
    llm_client,          # anthropic.AsyncAnthropic or mock callable
    model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6",
) -> tuple[ExtractedFields, list[str]]:
    """
    Use Claude vision to extract raw fields from an invoice image.
    Returns (ExtractedFields, issues).
    """
    issues: list[str] = []

    with open(image_ref.path, "rb") as f:
        image_data = base64.standard_b64encode(f.read()).decode("utf-8")

    media_type = image_ref.media_type
    # Anthropic accepts image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/webp
    if media_type not in ("image/jpeg", "image/png", "image/gif", "image/webp"):
        media_type = "image/png"

    response = await llm_client.messages.create(
        model=model,
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[
            {
                "role": "user",
                "content": [
                    {
                        "type": "image",
                        "source": {
                            "type": "base64",
                            "media_type": media_type,
                            "data": image_data,
                        },
                    },
                    {"type": "text", "text": EXTRACTION_PROMPT},
                ],
            }
        ],
    )

    raw = response.content[0].text.strip()
    # Strip markdown fences if model added them despite instructions
    raw = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", raw)
    raw = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", raw)

    try:
        data = json.loads(raw)
    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
        issues.append(f"{image_ref.filename}: could not parse extraction response — {e}")
        data = {}

    fields = ExtractedFields(
        vendor=data.get("vendor"),
        invoice_date=data.get("invoice_date"),
        invoice_number=data.get("invoice_number"),
        total=data.get("total"),
        currency=data.get("currency", "USD"),
        line_items=data.get("line_items", []),
        raw_notes=data.get("raw_notes", ""),
    )

    if not fields.vendor:
        issues.append(f"{image_ref.filename}: vendor name missing")
    if fields.total is None:
        issues.append(f"{image_ref.filename}: total amount missing")

    return fields, issues


# ── Tool 3: normalize_invoice ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

_DATE_FORMATS = [
    "%Y-%m-%d", "%m/%d/%Y", "%d/%m/%Y", "%B %d, %Y",
    "%b %d, %Y", "%d %B %Y", "%d %b %Y", "%Y%m%d",
]


def _parse_date(raw: Optional[str]) -> Optional[date]:
    if not raw:
        return None
    raw = raw.strip()
    for fmt in _DATE_FORMATS:
        try:
            return datetime.strptime(raw, fmt).date()
        except ValueError:
            continue
    return None


def _usd_amount(total: Optional[float], currency: str) -> float:
    """Trivial pass-through — real FX conversion would go here."""
    if total is None:
        return 0.0
    return round(float(total), 2)


async def normalize_invoice(
    fields: ExtractedFields,
    image_ref: ImageRef,
) -> tuple[NormalizedInvoice, list[str]]:
    """
    Standardize raw extracted fields (dates, currency, vendor name).
    Pure Python — no LLM call.
    """
    issues: list[str] = []

    parsed_date = _parse_date(fields.invoice_date)
    if fields.invoice_date and not parsed_date:
        issues.append(f"{image_ref.filename}: unrecognized date format '{fields.invoice_date}'")

    vendor = (fields.vendor or "Unknown Vendor").strip().title()
    total_usd = _usd_amount(fields.total, fields.currency)

    if total_usd == 0.0:
        issues.append(f"{image_ref.filename}: total is zero or missing — included as $0.00")

    return NormalizedInvoice(
        image_ref=image_ref,
        vendor=vendor,
        invoice_date=parsed_date,
        invoice_number=fields.invoice_number,
        total_usd=total_usd,
        currency=fields.currency,
        line_items=fields.line_items,
    ), issues


# ── Tool 4: categorize_invoice ────────────────────────────────────────────────

CATEGORIZE_PROMPT = """You are an expense categorization assistant.

Given this invoice summary, assign exactly one expense category.

Invoice:
  Vendor: {vendor}
  Date: {date}
  Total: {total} {currency}
  Line items: {line_items}

Allowed categories:
{categories}

{prompt_hint}

Return ONLY a JSON object:
{{
  "category": "one of the allowed categories above",
  "confidence": 0.0 to 1.0,
  "notes": "brief explanation"
}}
No markdown fences."""


async def categorize_invoice(
    invoice: NormalizedInvoice,
    allowed_categories: list[str],
    llm_client,
    model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6",
    prompt_hint: str = "",
) -> CategorizedInvoice:
    """
    Use Claude to assign an expense category with confidence score.
    """
    categories_str = "\n".join(f"- {c}" for c in allowed_categories)
    hint_section = f"\nAdditional instructions: {prompt_hint}" if prompt_hint else ""

    user_msg = CATEGORIZE_PROMPT.format(
        vendor=invoice.vendor,
        date=str(invoice.invoice_date) if invoice.invoice_date else "unknown",
        total=invoice.total_usd,
        currency=invoice.currency,
        line_items=", ".join(invoice.line_items) if invoice.line_items else "not specified",
        categories=categories_str,
        prompt_hint=hint_section,
    )

    response = await llm_client.messages.create(
        model=model,
        max_tokens=256,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_msg}],
    )

    raw = response.content[0].text.strip()
    raw = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", raw)
    raw = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", raw)

    try:
        data = json.loads(raw)
        category = data.get("category", "Other")
        confidence = float(data.get("confidence", 0.5))
        notes = data.get("notes", "")
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
        category = "Other"
        confidence = 0.3
        notes = "Categorization response could not be parsed; defaulted to Other."

    # Guard: ensure category is in allowed list
    if category not in allowed_categories:
        notes = f"Model returned '{category}' (not in allowed list); defaulted to Other. {notes}"
        category = "Other"
        confidence = min(confidence, 0.4)

    return CategorizedInvoice(
        image_ref=invoice.image_ref,
        vendor=invoice.vendor,
        invoice_date=invoice.invoice_date,
        invoice_number=invoice.invoice_number,
        total_usd=invoice.total_usd,
        currency=invoice.currency,
        category=category,
        confidence=confidence,
        notes=notes,
    )


# ── Tool 5: aggregate ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def aggregate(invoices: list[CategorizedInvoice]) -> Aggregates:
    """
    Sum totals across all invoices and group by expense category.
    Pure Python — no LLM call.
    """
    by_category: dict[str, float] = {}
    total = 0.0

    for inv in invoices:
        total += inv.total_usd
        by_category[inv.category] = round(
            by_category.get(inv.category, 0.0) + inv.total_usd, 2
        )

    return Aggregates(
        total_spend=round(total, 2),
        by_category=by_category,
        invoice_count=len(invoices),
    )


# ── Tool 6: generate_report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def generate_report(
    aggregates: Aggregates,
    invoices: list[CategorizedInvoice],
    issues: list[str],
    run_id: str,
) -> FinalReport:
    """
    Assemble the final structured report.
    Pure Python — no LLM call.
    """
    return FinalReport(
        run_id=run_id,
        summary=aggregates,
        invoices=invoices,
        issues=issues,
    )

"""
Tool registry — the 6 tools the agent may use to process invoices.

All tools are plain async functions. The agent graph calls them directly
so we keep full control of SSE event emission timing.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import base64
import json
import os
import re
import tempfile
from datetime import date, datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import aiofiles
from fastapi import UploadFile

from agent.state import (
    Aggregates,
    CategorizedInvoice,
    ExtractedFields,
    FinalReport,
    ImageRef,
    NormalizedInvoice,
)


# ── Tool 1: load_images ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def load_images(files: list[UploadFile], tmp_dir: str) -> list[ImageRef]:
    """
    Save uploaded files to a temp directory and return ImageRef objects.
    """
    refs: list[ImageRef] = []
    for upload in files:
        data = await upload.read()
        filename = upload.filename or "invoice.png"
        dest = Path(tmp_dir) / filename
        # Handle name collisions
        counter = 1
        while dest.exists():
            stem, suffix = os.path.splitext(filename)
            dest = Path(tmp_dir) / f"{stem}_{counter}{suffix}"
            counter += 1

        async with aiofiles.open(dest, "wb") as f:
            await f.write(data)

        refs.append(ImageRef(
            path=str(dest),
            filename=dest.name,
            size_bytes=len(data),
            media_type=upload.content_type or "image/png",
        ))
    return refs


# ── Tool 2: extract_invoice_fields ────────────────────────────────────────────

EXTRACTION_PROMPT = """You are an invoice data extractor. Analyze the invoice image and return a JSON object with these fields:
{
  "vendor": "Company name on the invoice",
  "invoice_date": "Date as YYYY-MM-DD or null",
  "invoice_number": "Invoice/receipt number or null",
  "total": numeric total amount (float) or null,
  "currency": "3-letter ISO currency code, default USD",
  "line_items": ["list of line item descriptions"],
  "raw_notes": "anything unusual or ambiguous you noticed"
}
Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences."""


async def extract_invoice_fields(
    image_ref: ImageRef,
    llm_client,          # anthropic.AsyncAnthropic or mock callable
    model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6",
) -> tuple[ExtractedFields, list[str]]:
    """
    Use Claude vision to extract raw fields from an invoice image.
    Returns (ExtractedFields, issues).
    """
    issues: list[str] = []

    with open(image_ref.path, "rb") as f:
        image_data = base64.standard_b64encode(f.read()).decode("utf-8")

    media_type = image_ref.media_type
    # Anthropic accepts image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/webp
    if media_type not in ("image/jpeg", "image/png", "image/gif", "image/webp"):
        media_type = "image/png"

    response = await llm_client.messages.create(
        model=model,
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[
            {
                "role": "user",
                "content": [
                    {
                        "type": "image",
                        "source": {
                            "type": "base64",
                            "media_type": media_type,
                            "data": image_data,
                        },
                    },
                    {"type": "text", "text": EXTRACTION_PROMPT},
                ],
            }
        ],
    )

    raw = response.content[0].text.strip()
    # Strip markdown fences if model added them despite instructions
    raw = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", raw)
    raw = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", raw)

    try:
        data = json.loads(raw)
    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
        issues.append(f"{image_ref.filename}: could not parse extraction response — {e}")
        data = {}

    fields = ExtractedFields(
        vendor=data.get("vendor"),
        invoice_date=data.get("invoice_date"),
        invoice_number=data.get("invoice_number"),
        total=data.get("total"),
        currency=data.get("currency", "USD"),
        line_items=data.get("line_items", []),
        raw_notes=data.get("raw_notes", ""),
    )

    if not fields.vendor:
        issues.append(f"{image_ref.filename}: vendor name missing")
    if fields.total is None:
        issues.append(f"{image_ref.filename}: total amount missing")

    return fields, issues


# ── Tool 3: normalize_invoice ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

_DATE_FORMATS = [
    "%Y-%m-%d", "%m/%d/%Y", "%d/%m/%Y", "%B %d, %Y",
    "%b %d, %Y", "%d %B %Y", "%d %b %Y", "%Y%m%d",
]


def _parse_date(raw: Optional[str]) -> Optional[date]:
    if not raw:
        return None
    raw = raw.strip()
    for fmt in _DATE_FORMATS:
        try:
            return datetime.strptime(raw, fmt).date()
        except ValueError:
            continue
    return None


def _usd_amount(total: Optional[float], currency: str) -> float:
    """Trivial pass-through — real FX conversion would go here."""
    if total is None:
        return 0.0
    return round(float(total), 2)


async def normalize_invoice(
    fields: ExtractedFields,
    image_ref: ImageRef,
) -> tuple[NormalizedInvoice, list[str]]:
    """
    Standardize raw extracted fields (dates, currency, vendor name).
    Pure Python — no LLM call.
    """
    issues: list[str] = []

    parsed_date = _parse_date(fields.invoice_date)
    if fields.invoice_date and not parsed_date:
        issues.append(f"{image_ref.filename}: unrecognized date format '{fields.invoice_date}'")

    vendor = (fields.vendor or "Unknown Vendor").strip().title()
    total_usd = _usd_amount(fields.total, fields.currency)

    if total_usd == 0.0:
        issues.append(f"{image_ref.filename}: total is zero or missing — included as $0.00")

    return NormalizedInvoice(
        image_ref=image_ref,
        vendor=vendor,
        invoice_date=parsed_date,
        invoice_number=fields.invoice_number,
        total_usd=total_usd,
        currency=fields.currency,
        line_items=fields.line_items,
    ), issues


# ── Tool 4: categorize_invoice ────────────────────────────────────────────────

CATEGORIZE_PROMPT = """You are an expense categorization assistant.

Given this invoice summary, assign exactly one expense category.

Invoice:
  Vendor: {vendor}
  Date: {date}
  Total: {total} {currency}
  Line items: {line_items}

Allowed categories:
{categories}

{prompt_hint}

Return ONLY a JSON object:
{{
  "category": "one of the allowed categories above",
  "confidence": 0.0 to 1.0,
  "notes": "brief explanation"
}}
No markdown fences."""


async def categorize_invoice(
    invoice: NormalizedInvoice,
    allowed_categories: list[str],
    llm_client,
    model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6",
    prompt_hint: str = "",
) -> CategorizedInvoice:
    """
    Use Claude to assign an expense category with confidence score.
    """
    categories_str = "\n".join(f"- {c}" for c in allowed_categories)
    hint_section = f"\nAdditional instructions: {prompt_hint}" if prompt_hint else ""

    user_msg = CATEGORIZE_PROMPT.format(
        vendor=invoice.vendor,
        date=str(invoice.invoice_date) if invoice.invoice_date else "unknown",
        total=invoice.total_usd,
        currency=invoice.currency,
        line_items=", ".join(invoice.line_items) if invoice.line_items else "not specified",
        categories=categories_str,
        prompt_hint=hint_section,
    )

    response = await llm_client.messages.create(
        model=model,
        max_tokens=256,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_msg}],
    )

    raw = response.content[0].text.strip()
    raw = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", raw)
    raw = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", raw)

    try:
        data = json.loads(raw)
        category = data.get("category", "Other")
        confidence = float(data.get("confidence", 0.5))
        notes = data.get("notes", "")
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
        category = "Other"
        confidence = 0.3
        notes = "Categorization response could not be parsed; defaulted to Other."

    # Guard: ensure category is in allowed list
    if category not in allowed_categories:
        notes = f"Model returned '{category}' (not in allowed list); defaulted to Other. {notes}"
        category = "Other"
        confidence = min(confidence, 0.4)

    return CategorizedInvoice(
        image_ref=invoice.image_ref,
        vendor=invoice.vendor,
        invoice_date=invoice.invoice_date,
        invoice_number=invoice.invoice_number,
        total_usd=invoice.total_usd,
        currency=invoice.currency,
        category=category,
        confidence=confidence,
        notes=notes,
    )


# ── Tool 5: aggregate ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def aggregate(invoices: list[CategorizedInvoice]) -> Aggregates:
    """
    Sum totals across all invoices and group by expense category.
    Pure Python — no LLM call.
    """
    by_category: dict[str, float] = {}
    total = 0.0

    for inv in invoices:
        total += inv.total_usd
        by_category[inv.category] = round(
            by_category.get(inv.category, 0.0) + inv.total_usd, 2
        )

    return Aggregates(
        total_spend=round(total, 2),
        by_category=by_category,
        invoice_count=len(invoices),
    )


# ── Tool 6: generate_report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def generate_report(
    aggregates: Aggregates,
    invoices: list[CategorizedInvoice],
    issues: list[str],
    run_id: str,
) -> FinalReport:
    """
    Assemble the final structured report.
    Pure Python — no LLM call.
    """
    return FinalReport(
        run_id=run_id,
        summary=aggregates,
        invoices=invoices,
        issues=issues,
    )

"""
Mock LLM responses for MOCK_MODE=true.

Provides deterministic fake invoice data so the entire pipeline can be
demonstrated without any API keys or network access.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from datetime import date
from typing import Optional

from agent.state import (
    CategorizedInvoice,
    ExtractedFields,
    ImageRef,
    NormalizedInvoice,
)

# Rotating pool of realistic fake invoices
_MOCK_INVOICES = [
    {
        "vendor": "Delta Air Lines",
        "invoice_date": "2024-11-15",
        "invoice_number": "DLTA-20241115-887",
        "total": 1240.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "line_items": ["Round-trip flight NYC→SFO", "Seat upgrade"],
        "raw_notes": "Business travel receipt",
        "category": "Travel (air/hotel)",
        "confidence": 0.97,
        "notes": "Airline receipt — clearly travel.",
    },
    {
        "vendor": "GitHub Inc",
        "invoice_date": "2024-11-01",
        "invoice_number": "GH-INV-2024-11",
        "total": 21.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "line_items": ["GitHub Copilot Business subscription"],
        "raw_notes": "",
        "category": "Software / Subscriptions",
        "confidence": 0.99,
        "notes": "Monthly SaaS subscription.",
    },
    {
        "vendor": "Nobu Restaurant",
        "invoice_date": "2024-11-20",
        "invoice_number": "NOBU-44512",
        "total": 387.50,
        "currency": "USD",
        "line_items": ["Client dinner x4", "Wine pairing"],
        "raw_notes": "Dinner with Acme Corp client",
        "category": "Meals & Entertainment",
        "confidence": 0.95,
        "notes": "Restaurant receipt with client notation.",
    },
    {
        "vendor": "Deloitte LLP",
        "invoice_date": "2024-10-31",
        "invoice_number": "DLT-2024-Q4-001",
        "total": 8500.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "line_items": ["Tax advisory services — October 2024"],
        "raw_notes": "",
        "category": "Professional Services",
        "confidence": 0.96,
        "notes": "Consulting/advisory firm invoice.",
    },
    {
        "vendor": "Staples Business Advantage",
        "invoice_date": "2024-11-10",
        "invoice_number": "SBA-9923411",
        "total": 134.75,
        "currency": "USD",
        "line_items": ["Printer paper (2 cases)", "Ballpoint pens x24", "Folders"],
        "raw_notes": "",
        "category": "Office Supplies",
        "confidence": 0.98,
        "notes": "Standard office supply purchase.",
    },
]


class _MockMessage:
    """Mimics anthropic.types.Message structure."""
    def __init__(self, text: str):
        self.content = [_MockContent(text)]
        self.model = "mock-claude"
        self.usage = None


class _MockContent:
    def __init__(self, text: str):
        self.text = text
        self.type = "text"


class MockAnthropicClient:
    """
    Drop-in replacement for anthropic.AsyncAnthropic.
    Rotates through _MOCK_INVOICES based on a counter so each uploaded
    image gets a different fake invoice.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self._counter = 0
        self.messages = _MockMessages(self)

    def next_invoice(self) -> dict:
        inv = _MOCK_INVOICES[self._counter % len(_MOCK_INVOICES)]
        self._counter += 1
        return inv


class _MockMessages:
    def __init__(self, client: MockAnthropicClient):
        self._client = client

    async def create(self, model: str, max_tokens: int, messages: list, **kwargs) -> _MockMessage:
        import json
        await asyncio.sleep(0.05)  # simulate minimal latency

        inv = self._client.next_invoice()
        # Determine which tool called us by checking the message content
        user_content = messages[-1]["content"]
        if isinstance(user_content, list):
            # Extraction call (vision message with image block)
            payload = {
                "vendor": inv["vendor"],
                "invoice_date": inv["invoice_date"],
                "invoice_number": inv["invoice_number"],
                "total": inv["total"],
                "currency": inv["currency"],
                "line_items": inv["line_items"],
                "raw_notes": inv["raw_notes"],
            }
            # Back up counter — categorize will increment again for same invoice
            self._client._counter -= 1
        else:
            # Categorization call (text message)
            payload = {
                "category": inv["category"],
                "confidence": inv["confidence"],
                "notes": inv["notes"],
            }

        return _MockMessage(json.dumps(payload))

"""
LLM client factory.

Returns either a real anthropic.AsyncAnthropic client or the mock
client, based on config.settings.mock_mode.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from config import settings


def get_llm_client():
    """
    Return the appropriate LLM client based on MOCK_MODE setting.

    In real mode: anthropic.AsyncAnthropic (uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).
    In mock mode: MockAnthropicClient (no API calls, no key needed).
    """
    if settings.mock_mode:
        from agent.mock import MockAnthropicClient
        return MockAnthropicClient()

    import anthropic
    if not settings.anthropic_api_key:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set. "
            "Either set the key or enable MOCK_MODE=true."
        )
    return anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(api_key=settings.anthropic_api_key)

"""
LLM client factory.

Returns either a real anthropic.AsyncAnthropic client or the mock
client, based on config.settings.mock_mode.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from config import settings


def get_llm_client():
    """
    Return the appropriate LLM client based on MOCK_MODE setting.

    In real mode: anthropic.AsyncAnthropic (uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).
    In mock mode: MockAnthropicClient (no API calls, no key needed).
    """
    if settings.mock_mode:
        from agent.mock import MockAnthropicClient
        return MockAnthropicClient()

    import anthropic
    if not settings.anthropic_api_key:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set. "
            "Either set the key or enable MOCK_MODE=true."
        )
    return anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(api_key=settings.anthropic_api_key)

input.content:
"""
SSE event types and queue-based emitter.

All graph nodes receive an EventEmitter via RunnableConfig.configurable
and call emit() to push events into the asyncio.Queue that the SSE
endpoint drains.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import json
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any, Optional


# ── Event payload builders ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _now() -> str:
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()


def evt_run_started(run_id: str, invoice_count: int, mock_mode: bool) -> dict:
    return {
        "event": "run_started",
        "run_id": run_id,
        "timestamp": _now(),
        "invoice_count": invoice_count,
        "mock_mode": mock_mode,
    }


def evt_progress(run_id: str, step: str, message: str, current: int = 0, total: int = 0) -> dict:
    return {
        "event": "progress",
        "run_id": run_id,
        "timestamp": _now(),
        "step": step,
        "message": message,
        "current": current,
        "total": total,
    }


def evt_tool_call(run_id: str, tool: str, input_summary: dict) -> dict:
    return {
        "event": "tool_call",
        "run_id": run_id,
        "timestamp": _now(),
        "tool": tool,
        "input_summary": input_summary,
    }


def evt_tool_result(run_id: str, tool: str, output_summary: dict, duration_ms: int) -> dict:
    return {
        "event": "tool_result",
        "run_id": run_id,
        "timestamp": _now(),
        "tool": tool,
        "output_summary": output_summary,
        "duration_ms": duration_ms,
    }


def evt_invoice_result(run_id: str, index: int, filename: str, data: dict) -> dict:
    return {
        "event": "invoice_result",
        "run_id": run_id,
        "timestamp": _now(),
        "index": index,
        "filename": filename,
        "data": data,
    }


def evt_final_result(run_id: str, summary: dict, invoices: list[dict], issues: list[str]) -> dict:
    return {
        "event": "final_result",
        "run_id": run_id,
        "timestamp": _now(),
        "summary": summary,
        "invoices": invoices,
        "issues": issues,
    }


def evt_error(run_id: str, message: str, step: str = "", recoverable: bool = False) -> dict:
    return {
        "event": "error",
        "run_id": run_id,
        "timestamp": _now(),
        "message": message,
        "step": step,
        "recoverable": recoverable,
    }


# ── SSE formatting ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def to_sse(payload: dict) -> str:
    """Format a dict as an SSE data line."""
    return f"data: {json.dumps(payload)}\n\n"


# ── EventEmitter ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class EventEmitter:
    """
    Thin wrapper around an asyncio.Queue.
    Graph nodes call emit() to push SSE event dicts.
    The SSE endpoint drains the queue.
    """

    SENTINEL = object()  # signals end of stream

    def __init__(self):
        self.queue: asyncio.Queue[Any] = asyncio.Queue()

    async def emit(self, payload: dict) -> None:
        await self.queue.put(payload)

    async def close(self) -> None:
        await self.queue.put(self.SENTINEL)

    async def __aiter__(self):
        while True:
            item = await self.queue.get()
            if item is self.SENTINEL:
                break
            yield item
